description: Machine-readable physics rules for rFactor 2 setup analysis. Used by validateRecommendation() to verify LLM output against known vehicle dynamics.

tire_pressure.physics: Excessive pressure concentrates contact patch at center; reduces total grip area and increases center wear.
Under-pressure causes tire to bulge at edges; center runs cold, losing grip.

tire_pressure.if: Front tire temps consistently > Rear tire temps
tire_pressure.then: Rebalance load: lower front pressure OR increase rear pressure
tire_pressure.physics: Front overworked relative to rear; equalizing thermal load improves overall grip.

tire_pressure.if: Tire_Temp_Delta(LapN - Lap1) > 15°C without track temp change
tire_pressure.then: Pressure setting outside optimal window — adjust toward target
tire_pressure.physics: Progressive heat buildup over laps indicates thermal instability from incorrect pressure.

tire_camber.physics: Outer edge overheating means insufficient camber for lateral loads; more negative camber distributes contact patch inward.
Excessive negative camber concentrates load on inner edge; reducing magnitude widens contact patch.
Extreme negative camber reduces straight-line braking contact patch, causing instability under heavy braking.

tire_camber.if: Camber made more negative by 1.5° AND Grip_Fract increases
tire_camber.physics: Negative camber typically beneficial up to ~-3.5° for most tire constructions; beyond that, diminishing returns.

tire_saturation.physics: Front at saturation, rear underutilized — unstable state with high oversteer risk.

tire_saturation.then: Reduce rear load or increase mechanical grip
tire_saturation.physics: Rear tires beyond saturation point means traction loss risk; either reduce demand or increase capacity.

tire_saturation.then: Check for insufficient downforce or cold tire condition
tire_saturation.physics: Available grip not exploited suggests thermal or aerodynamic root cause rather than mechanical setup.
Physically impossible grip fraction >1.0 indicates sensor/scaling issue in telemetry data.

suspension.physics: Risk of chassis bottoming — contact with surface destroys aero seal and damages floor.

suspension.if: Ride_Height variation under braking > 5mm vs acceleration
suspension.then: Stiffen front spring OR increase front slow bump damper
suspension.physics: Excessive nose dive under braking means weight transfer too aggressive; stiffer front resists compression.

suspension.then: INCREASE ARB stiffness (front or rear depending on balance)
suspension.physics: Excessive roll reduces aerodynamic efficiency and tire contact consistency.

suspension.then: Normalize rake — lower front or raise rear
suspension.physics: Extreme rake increases rear load bias, can promote understeer and ground effect instability.

suspension.if: G_Force_Long high (braking) AND large front ride height variation
suspension.then: Front springs too soft OR bump damping insufficient
suspension.physics: Braking load causes excessive front compression; stiffer springs or dampers resist dive.

suspension.if: Ride height oscillates ±5mm between consecutive corners
suspension.physics: Under-damped extension phase causes suspension ringing — car doesn't settle between inputs.

suspension.if: G-force spike at corner entry AND sharp ride height drop AND slow recovery
suspension.physics: Insufficient slow compression damping fails to control braking-phase weight transfer.

suspension.if: Damper rebound increased AND ride height becomes erratic
suspension.then: Decrease fast rebound; increase slow rebound (split tuning)
suspension.physics: Over-stiff rebound at all speeds bounces suspension; split damping treats transient and steady differently.

suspension.if: Grip_Fract drops after first lap AND ride height degradation observed
suspension.physics: Track heat increases compliance; stiffer damping holds geometry as conditions evolve.

suspension.if: Spring_Rate increased 20% AND ride height increases >2mm
suspension.physics: Stiffer springs reduce compression; ride height should decrease or stay constant, not increase.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Understeer at corner entry/mid: Grip_Front > Grip_Rear
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Soften front ARB OR stiffen rear ARB
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Reducing front roll stiffness relative to rear transfers lateral load distribution rearward, freeing front grip.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Oversteer at corner mid/exit: Grip_Rear > Grip_Front
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Soften rear ARB OR stiffen front ARB
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Reducing rear roll stiffness stabilizes the rear by allowing more rear grip under lateral load.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Understeer at entry → oversteer at exit in same corner
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Damper tuning issue, not ARB alone — investigate transient response
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Entry/exit split behavior indicates transient vs steady-state imbalance; ARBs address steady state only.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Understeer proportional to speed (worse at higher speeds)
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Aerodynamic imbalance, not mechanical — address wing settings
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Speed-dependent handling is dominated by aerodynamic forces, not mechanical grip.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Body roll > 2.5° in fast corners (>150 km/h)
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: INCREASE ARB stiffness (front or rear or both)
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Excessive roll at high speed reduces effective downforce and degrades tire contact angle.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Exit oversteer: rear loose on throttle application
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Stiffen rear spring OR increase diff lock
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Rear unloads under acceleration; stiffer spring resists squat and diff lock couples wheel speeds.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Exit understeer: car pushes on throttle application
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Soften front spring OR soften front ARB
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Front remains loaded on exit preventing nose rotation; softening front allows weight transfer rearward.

anti_roll_bar_balance.if: Mid-corner understeer at low speed only (not high speed)
anti_roll_bar_balance.then: Soften front ARB or reduce diff preload
anti_roll_bar_balance.physics: Low-speed understeer is mechanical (not aero); ARB and diff preload adjustments address mechanical balance.

aerodynamics.if: Understeer in fast corners (>150 km/h) AND low rear wing angle
aerodynamics.physics: More rear downforce shifts aerodynamic balance rearward, reducing high-speed understeer.

aerodynamics.if: Oversteer at high speed (not present at low speed)
aerodynamics.then: REDUCE front wing OR INCREASE rear wing
aerodynamics.physics: Speed-dependent oversteer indicates front-biased aerodynamic balance producing excess front grip.

aerodynamics.then: Increase rear wing or reduce front wing to approach 50/50
aerodynamics.physics: Ideal aero balance targets ~50/50 for neutral high-speed handling; front bias causes oversteer.

aerodynamics.if: Top speed deficit on straight AND high wing angles (both >5°)
aerodynamics.physics: High drag penalty from excessive wing angles exceeds aero grip benefit on straights.

aerodynamics.if: Rear wing > +5° AND measured downforce < baseline
aerodynamics.then: Possible wing stall — reduce angle and verify efficiency
aerodynamics.physics: Excessive wing angle can cause airflow separation (stall), reducing downforce despite higher geometric angle.

aerodynamics.if: Understeer in fast corners AND rear wing already high (>6°)
aerodynamics.then: Root cause is likely mechanical, not aero — investigate springs/ARB
aerodynamics.physics: When aero is already maximized, further wing increases have diminishing returns; mechanical setup is limiting factor.

aerodynamics.if: Aero balance shift applied AND ride height drops significantly
aerodynamics.then: Verify spring capacity for added aero load
aerodynamics.physics: More downforce compresses suspension; springs must support additional load to maintain target ride height.

aerodynamics.if: Wing change ≤1° AND predicted speed impact <2%
aerodynamics.then: Wing change justified — marginal drag acceptable
aerodynamics.physics: Small wing adjustments produce meaningful aero balance shift with negligible drag penalty.

braking.physics: Front brakes overheating while rear underutilized; rearward bias equalizes thermal load and improves deceleration.

braking.if: G_Force_Lat spikes detected during braking zones
braking.then: Check brake bias and rear spring stiffness
braking.physics: Combined lateral and longitudinal load under braking destabilizes rear; bias or spring adjustment needed.
Asymmetric front brake temps indicate caliper or disc mechanical problem, not a setup calibration issue.

braking.if: G_Force_Long peak low AND all brake temps < 300°C
braking.then: Insufficient brake pressure or duct cooling issue
braking.physics: Low deceleration with low temps means brakes aren't working hard enough — either pressure or cooling problem.

braking.if: Brake temps rise continuously without reaching plateau
braking.physics: Insufficient cooling airflow prevents thermal equilibrium; larger ducts dissipate heat faster.

braking.if: Brake_Temp > 450°C AND deceleration decreasing over laps
braking.physics: Brake fade threshold typically 400–450°C; beyond this, friction material effectiveness decreases nonlinearly.

braking.if: Front ride height dive > 15mm under heavy braking
braking.then: Front spring too soft for braking load
braking.physics: Excessive front dive reduces front brake effectiveness by changing caliper geometry and pad contact angle.
Pulsing deceleration indicates front/rear alternating lock; precise bias balances lock-up threshold.

differential.if: RL/RR wheel speed delta > 10 rad/s during traction application
differential.physics: Inside wheel spinning faster than outside means open diff losing torque to path of least resistance.

differential.if: Snap oversteer at corner exit AND diff lock > 70%
differential.physics: Over-locked diff forces both rear wheels to same speed, causing premature inside wheel slip on exit.

differential.if: Traction G_Long < 0.5g consistently AND no wheel lock events
differential.physics: Differential too open; not exploiting available traction from both driven wheels.

differential.if: Diff lock increased AND steering becomes noticeably heavy
differential.then: FLAG — verify lock isn't excessive for cornering
differential.physics: High differential lock reduces cornering compliance by forcing speed equality; compromises rotation.

differential.if: Corner exit understeer AND diff lock < 30%
differential.physics: More differential coupling helps rotate the car out of corners by reducing inside wheel slip.

validation.if: Recommendation says 'reduce rear wing to fix understeer'
validation.physics: Less rear downforce = less rear grip = MORE oversteer, not less understeer. This is a common LLM hallucination.

validation.if: Recommendation says 'increase front spring to improve traction on exit'
validation.physics: Front spring rate has no direct causal relationship to rear axle traction on corner exit.

validation.if: Recommendation says 'soften brake bias for mid-corner balance'
validation.physics: Brake bias affects braking phase only; it has no impact on steady-state mid-corner balance.

validation.if: Recommendation softens BOTH front AND rear ARB simultaneously
validation.then: REJECT — symmetrical change doesn't fix balance
validation.physics: Balance is a relative concept; changing both ARBs in the same direction doesn't shift balance point.

validation.if: Reason cites a numeric value not present in the telemetry input
validation.physics: LLM fabricated a telemetry value; data must be traceable to actual input.

validation.if: Reason says 'increase' but new_value < old_value (or vice versa)
validation.physics: The stated direction of change contradicts the actual numeric change applied.

validation.if: Recommendation contains driving advice ('reduce speed', 'brake earlier') instead of setup change
validation.physics: Setup recommendations must propose parameter changes, not driving technique changes.

validation.if: Recommendation says 'increase front downforce to reduce oversteer'
validation.physics: More front downforce shifts balance forward = more front grip relative to rear = worsens oversteer.

validation.if: Parameter proposed is present in fixed_params list
validation.physics: User has explicitly locked this parameter; it must not be modified regardless of analysis.

validation.if: new_value is expressed in clicks/steps instead of physical units
validation.physics: Setup values must use physical units (kPa, deg, N/mm, etc.), never click counts or step indices.
